title: FEMS pinned core memory: pinned=true items always included before scoring (folded WP-1-FEMS-Pinned-Core-Memory)
goal: Add `pinned: bool` flag to MemoryItem and ensure pinned items are always included in every MemoryPack before the scoring formula runs on the remaining budget. Ports the Letta/MemGPT 'core memory' pattern. Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Pinned-Core-Memory. Protects operator-marked memories from outcome-loop pruning (MT-158) and from displacement by high-recency low-importance items.
expected_diff_shape: Extends src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/fems_mt_handoff_memory_context.rs (in main) with `pinned: bool` MemoryItem field. New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/pinned_core.rs (PinnedCoreSelector + PinError types + PinIpc Tauri commands). Postgres migration adds pinned column with index. Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/pinned_core_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-141 (MemoryCapsule), MT-143 (CapsuleBuilder), MT-158 (outcome feedback - pinned items must be protected from outcome-driven score decay).
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Pinned-Core-Memory preserved_intent. Surface: extends MemoryItem with `pub pinned: bool` (default false). PinnedCoreSelector: `pub fn select_pack_with_pins(items: &[MemoryItem], budget: MemoryPackBudgets) -> Result<MemoryPack, PinError>` which (1) takes all pinned items first, (2) checks they fit budget (returns PinError::PinnedExceedsBudget if not), (3) runs the existing scoring formula on remaining items with remaining budget, (4) merges into single ordered MemoryPack with pinned items first. The CapsuleBuilder (MT-143) MUST consume this selector instead of the bare scoring path. Outcome feedback tuner (MT-158) MUST skip pinned items when applying score decay (assert via test). IPC: Tauri commands `kernel.memory_pin.set(item_id, pinned: bool, reason: String) -> PinReceipt`, `kernel.memory_pin.list() -> Vec<PinnedItem>`. All pin/unpin flows through KernelActionCatalogV1 (PinMemoryItem / UnpinMemoryItem action variants) and emits FR-EVT-MEMORY-PIN / FR-EVT-MEMORY-UNPIN. Postgres migration: ADD COLUMN pinned BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE; CREATE INDEX ON memory_item (pinned) WHERE pinned = TRUE. Tests: pinned items always selected first; budget overflow with pinned-only returns PinError; outcome feedback skips pinned items in tuning; pin/unpin IPC routes through catalog; FR events emitted exactly once per mutation.
risk_if_missed: Without pinned core memory, operator-marked critical memories (preferences, safety constraints, project identity) can be displaced by high-recency noise; outcome feedback can prune them entirely.
validator_focus: Pinned items always included first; outcome feedback respects pin flag; pin/unpin flows through KernelActionCatalogV1.

minimum_controls: Pinned items always included before scoring; tested for budget-edge and overflow cases.
Outcome feedback tuner skips pinned items so a temporary bad outcome cannot demote a pinned safety constraint.
Pin/unpin flows through KernelActionCatalogV1 with typed FR events.
